name: Cerro de la Degollada – circular from Priego
name_original: Cerro de la Degollada – circular desde Priego
region_intro: Hiking around Priego de Córdoba offers a diverse landscape characterized by river valleys, hills, and natural pools. The region features varied terrain, including paths along the Escabas River and ascents to viewpoints like Mirador del Rodenal. These routes provide opportunities to explore the natural beauty of Priego, with options ranging from gentle riverside walks to more challenging hill climbs. Many trails are circular walks, making them convenient for exploration. Best hiking trails around Priego The most popular hiking route is Natural pool of Cañamares - circular by the Escaba river and La Dehesa camping, an easy 2.1 miles (3.4 km) trail that takes 59 minutes to complete, featuring a natural pool and riverside paths. Another top favourite among local hikers is Cerro de la Degollada – circular from Priego, a moderate 5.9 miles (9.5 km) path. This circular route offers views from Cerro de la Degollada, providing a rewarding experience for those seeking elevated perspectives. Local hikers also love the Route of the Mirador del Rodenal (PR-CU 42) — circular from Priego, an 8.8 miles (14.1 km) trail leading through varied terrain to a key viewpoint, often completed in about 4 hours 19 minutes. Hiking around Priego is defined by river valleys, hills, and scenic viewpoints, with a network of circular walks offering options for different ability levels. The routes in Priego are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 160 hikers have used komoot to explore Priego's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Priego?
A: Priego offers a diverse selection of over 30 hiking trails. These routes cater to various skill levels, with 15 easy, 13 moderate, and 3 difficult options to explore.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Priego?
A: The ideal times for hiking in Priego are spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, the weather is generally mild and pleasant, perfect for enjoying the region's natural beauty. Summers can be very hot, so early mornings or late afternoons are recommended if hiking during July and August.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Priego?
A: Yes, Priego has 15 easy hiking trails perfect for beginners or families. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013693821/natural-pool-of-canamares-circular-by-the-escaba-river-and-la-dehesa-camping" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural pool of Cañamares - circular by the Escaba river and La Dehesa camping&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 2.1-mile (3.4 km) route that takes about an hour to complete and features a natural pool and riverside paths.

Q: Are there challenging hikes for experienced hikers in Priego?
A: For experienced hikers seeking a challenge, Priego offers 3 difficult routes. One such option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20135360" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Escabas River – Cerro de la Degollá loop from Priego&lt;/a&gt;, a demanding 9.3-mile (15 km) trail with significant elevation gain, offering a rewarding experience.

Q: Are there circular hiking routes in Priego?
A: Yes, many of the hiking trails around Priego are circular, offering convenient starting and ending points. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1010839472/cerro-de-la-degollada-circular-from-priego" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro de la Degollada – circular from Priego&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate 5.9-mile (9.5 km) loop that provides excellent views.

Q: Can I find hikes with waterfalls in Priego?
A: Yes, the region around Priego features natural attractions including waterfalls. You can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5404050" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Waterfall of the Hocino Gorge&lt;/a&gt;, which is part of the scenic gorges around Priego.

Q: Are there viewpoints on the hiking trails around Priego?
A: Absolutely. Priego's landscape is dotted with hills and offers several rewarding viewpoints. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1242031295/route-of-the-mirador-del-rodenal-pr-cu-42-circular-from-priego" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Mirador del Rodenal (PR-CU 42) — circular from Priego&lt;/a&gt; is specifically designed to lead you to a key panoramic spot, offering stunning vistas of the surrounding area.

Q: What kind of attractions can I see along the hiking trails in Priego?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, trails around Priego offer various points of interest. You can discover the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5404032" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cañamares natural pool&lt;/a&gt;, the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5650912" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerta del Infierno&lt;/a&gt;, or the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5412046" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Estrecho Bridge&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features the dramatic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5412048" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hoz del Conde&lt;/a&gt; gorge.

Q: Are the hiking trails in Priego dog-friendly?
A: Most hiking trails in natural areas around Priego are generally dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near livestock, and to carry water for both yourself and your pet. Always respect local regulations and clean up after your dog.

Q: Where can I park when hiking in Priego?
A: Many hiking routes around Priego, especially those starting from the town itself or popular natural areas, have designated parking spots nearby. It's advisable to check the specific route details on komoot for information on recommended starting points and parking availability.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in Priego?
A: The komoot community highly rates hiking in Priego, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 20 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscape, including river valleys, hills, and natural pools, as well as the variety of circular walks available for different ability levels.

Q: Are there any trails that follow the Escabas River?
A: Yes, the Escabas River is a prominent feature of the region, and several trails follow its course. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1243265910/ribera-del-escabas-y-cerroya-pr-cu-43-circular-desde-priego" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ribera del Escabas y Cerroya (PR-CU 43) — circular desde Priego&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 5.2-mile (8.4 km) route that offers beautiful riverside scenery.
tips: The Climb to Cerro de la Degollá takes place in the town of Priego, a municipality located north of the province of Cuenca.
Also known as the Torre de Despeñaperros, the ruins are the only ones still standing of the monumental complex that extended over vast areas in the Cuenca area.
